title: How to Use Enhanced Web Crawler in GoHighLevel — Better AI Answers
description: Learn how GoHighLevel's Enhanced Web Crawler extracts 50% more content from interactive websites. Master tabs, accordions, and lazy-loaded sections for bette...
html_content:
Your chatbot is only as smart as the information it learns from. If your AI agent can't read the tabs, accordions, or dynamically loaded content on your website, it's missing critical answers—and your customers are getting frustrated responses.

GoHighLevel's Enhanced Web Crawler solves this problem by extracting up to 50% more on-page content from interactive websites. Instead of scraping just static text, your Conversation AI now understands tabs, accordions, modals, and lazy-loaded sections—the same way a human would read your site.

This means better chatbot answers, higher customer satisfaction, and less support burden on your team. If you're running a digital marketing agency or managing customer conversations at scale, this feature is a game-changer. Ready to see the difference? Start your free 30-day GoHighLevel trial here—that's double the standard trial, no credit card required.

What Is the Enhanced Web Crawler?

The Enhanced Web Crawler is an upgraded engine inside GoHighLevel's AI Knowledge Base that intelligently explores dynamic site content. Unlike older crawlers that only grab visible, static text, this tool actively discovers and extracts hidden details stored in accordions, tabs, modals, and lazy-loaded sections.

Modern websites—especially those built with React, Vue, and Angular—load content dynamically. Traditional web scrapers miss this information entirely. The Enhanced Web Crawler doesn't. It extracts 30-50% more training data from these interactive sites, giving your Conversation AI the complete picture it needs to answer customer questions accurately.

In practical terms: when a customer asks your chatbot a question about something buried in a tab or accordion on your website, your AI actually knows the answer now—because it learned it during the crawl.

How the Enhanced Web Crawler Works with Interactive Elements

The magic happens in how the crawler handles interactive page elements that traditional scrapers ignore:

Tabs: When your website displays content in tabbed sections ("Features," "Pricing," "FAQ" tabs, for example), the crawler automatically clicks through each tab and extracts the content. Your chatbot learns everything in every tab, not just the default active tab.

Accordions: Expandable content sections are expanded automatically during the crawl. Whether it's a "Common Questions" accordion or collapsible product details, the crawler gets it all.

Lazy-Loaded Sections: Content that only loads when a user scrolls to it is now extracted. The crawler scrolls through your page and captures everything that appears during that process.

Modals and Pop-Ups: Even dialog boxes and modals are discovered and parsed, so no important information is left out of your knowledge base.

The result: your Conversation AI has access to nearly complete information about your business, products, services, and policies—everything a customer might ask about.

Why Your AI Chatbot Needs Content Extraction From Tabs and Accordions

If your chatbot is trained on incomplete website data, it fails in predictable ways:

Incomplete Answers: A customer asks, "What's included in the Pro plan?" Your chatbot can't answer because that information is in a tab it never learned from. Result: customer frustration, missed sales opportunity.

Lost Context: Accordions often hide important details about policies, guarantees, or processes. Without this information, your AI can't help with follow-up questions or objections.

Poor Training Data: AI quality depends entirely on training data quality. Incomplete crawls mean incomplete training, which means weaker responses.

Increased Support Load: When your chatbot can't answer questions, conversations escalate to humans. This defeats the purpose of AI automation.

The Enhanced Web Crawler eliminates these problems by ensuring your knowledge base captures every piece of on-page content—visible or hidden. Your chatbot becomes genuinely capable of handling customer inquiries without human intervention.

💡 Pro Tip

Combine the Enhanced Web Crawler with regular refresh schedules. Set your crawler to update your knowledge base weekly or monthly—this ensures your chatbot stays current as your website content changes. Changes to pricing, new FAQ answers, or updated policies are automatically fed to your AI.

How to Set Up the Enhanced Web Crawler in GoHighLevel

Setting up the Enhanced Web Crawler is straightforward. Here's the exact process:

Step 1: Access Your AI Knowledge Base
Log into GoHighLevel and navigate to your Conversation AI section. Find the Knowledge Base where you want to import content.

Step 2: Locate the Web Crawler Tool
In the Knowledge Base settings, find the "Web Crawler" or "Import Content" option. Look for the "Enhanced Web Crawler" option—it's often labeled as the upgraded version.

Step 3: Enter Your Domain
Paste your website URL (or specific page URL) into the "Enter Domain" field. You can crawl your entire site or target specific pages.

Step 4: Click Get Data
Hit the "Get Data" button. The Enhanced Web Crawler will begin automatically harvesting content, including interactive elements. This may take a few seconds to a few minutes depending on your site size.

Step 5: Review and Sync
GoHighLevel will show you a preview of extracted content. Review the data to ensure everything looks correct, then sync it to your Knowledge Base. Your chatbot now has access to all this information.

Step 6: Test Your Chatbot
Ask your chatbot test questions about content that was previously in tabs or accordions. You should see immediate improvement in answer accuracy.

Real-World Benefits for Digital Marketing Agencies

If you're running a GoHighLevel agency, the Enhanced Web Crawler unlocks serious client value:

Better Lead Qualification: Your client's chatbot can now answer detailed product questions, pricing questions, and feature comparisons without dropping the conversation. More leads stay engaged longer.

Reduced Support Costs: When a chatbot can answer 80% of common questions accurately, your client's support team handles only complex issues. That's real cost savings.

Faster Implementation: You can set up more sophisticated AI systems in less time. The crawler does the heavy lifting of content extraction automatically.

Higher Client Retention: Clients see measurable improvements in chatbot performance. That translates to longer contracts and upsell opportunities for additional AI features.

Competitive Advantage: Most agencies haven't explained the Enhanced Web Crawler to their clients yet. Positioning this feature in your proposals sets you apart.

Maximizing Crawler Performance: Best Practices

Organize Your Website Logically: The crawler works best when your website structure is clean. Use clear hierarchies, logical menu structures, and accessible pages. Avoid deep nesting of content.

Use Descriptive Page Titles and Headers: The crawler uses H1, H2, and page titles to understand content context. Write descriptive headers that clearly indicate what each section covers.

Test Before Full Crawl: Start by crawling a single important page. Verify the extracted content is accurate, then scale to full-site crawls.

Schedule Regular Updates: Set the crawler to refresh periodically. This keeps your knowledge base in sync with website changes.

Monitor Chatbot Conversations: Use GoHighLevel's conversation logs to identify questions the chatbot struggles with. These gaps often reveal missing or poorly extracted content—then re-crawl and retrain.

The Enhanced Web Crawler transforms how AI agents learn from your website. By capturing interactive content automatically, your chatbot becomes genuinely knowledgeable and capable of handling real customer conversations.
